message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Adding opt-in debug/trace logging to StreamListener (guarded DEBUG-level logs on start/end/flush/reset). Only touching dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py. Let me know if your feature also touches this file so we can split within it.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py,dspy/streaming/streamify.py
note: Implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat in StreamListener (streaming_listener.py only). I understand is_last_chunk/on_chunk may be your feature elsewhere (e.g. messages.py StreamResponse, streamify.py callback wiring) - I'll integrate with is_last_chunk defensively via getattr/hasattr so no hard dependency on your changes landing first.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both need streaming_listener.py (mine: debug logging in __init__/receive/flush/reset; yours: idle_timeout_s/tick()). Since it's the same file, let's serialize instead of splitting regions: I'll go first since my change is small and purely additive (new __init__ params + guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls at existing trace points, no new methods, no control-flow changes). I will NOT touch tick()/idle_timeout logic. I'll send DONE with a summary of exact lines changed, then you layer your tick()/idle_timeout_s work on top by re-reading the file. OK to proceed on that order?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both need this same file, so let's sequence instead of splitting by file. My changes are small and localized: (1) __init__ - add idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts attr, (2) receive() - one line to refresh self._last_chunk_ts on non-empty chunk, (3) new tick() method appended near end of class (before flush or after). I'll go first since it's a small diff, then send DONE. Please re-read the file after my DONE before adding your debug logging so you don't clobber my edits (and I won't touch your log lines). Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed - you go first with idle_timeout_s/tick() work, send DONE when ready. I'll wait, then re-read the file and layer in my debug logging without touching your tick()/idle_timeout code. Sounds good.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Accepted: you go first with additive debug logging (no control-flow changes), send DONE with summary of changed lines, then I'll re-read the file and layer idle_timeout_s/tick() on top. I won't touch logging lines, you won't touch tick()/idle_timeout logic.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Our last two messages crossed (each said 'you go first'). Tiebreak: I will go first now (per your latest message), implement guarded debug logging only, then send DONE with exact line summary. Please hold off editing until you see my DONE.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished idle_timeout_s/tick() work. Changes: (1) added 'import time'; (2) __init__ - new idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts attr (docstring updated); (3) receive() - one added block 'if chunk_message: self._last_chunk_ts = time.time()' right after the try/except that extracts chunk_message, before the start_identifier cache-hit check; (4) new tick(now_ts=None) method inserted between receive() and flush(). tick() returns None if idle_timeout_s is None/stream not started/already ended; otherwise emits StreamResponse(chunk='') (tries is_last_chunk=True kwarg, falls back via TypeError if that field doesn't exist yet) and unconditionally resets stream_start/stream_end/cache_hit/field_start_queue/field_end_queue/_last_chunk_ts. No other lines touched. Safe for you to layer debug logging on top now - go ahead and re-read the file.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Debug logging done, purely additive, no control-flow changes. Summary of edits: (1) added 'import logging' + module-level _DEBUG_PREVIEW_MAX_LEN=80 + _truncate_preview() helper near top of file, after ADAPTER_SUPPORT_STREAMING. (2) __init__ signature: added debug: bool=False, debug_logger: logging.Logger|None=None params (appended after allow_reuse); added self.debug and self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger('dspy.streaming.listener') at the end of __init__ body. (3) In receive(): added one guarded self._logger.debug(...) call inside 'if self.allow_reuse:' reset block (after the 5 state-clearing lines, still inside that if-block) - logs 'State reset...'. (4) Added guarded log right after 'self.stream_end = True' in the cache_hit branch (~7 lines below start of receive) - logs 'Start detection...'. (5) Added guarded log right after the JSONAdapter leading-quote-strip line inside the 'if start_identifier in concat_message:' block - logs 'Start detection...'. (6) Added guarded log right before 'self.stream_end = True' in the rolling-end-check block (where re.search(end_identifier, concat_message) matches) - logs 'Rolling end check...'. (7) Added guarded log right before 'return StreamResponse(...)' at the end of receive() - logs 'Emit chunk...'. (8) In flush(): changed the three adapter branches from  to , then added a guarded log + single  at the end of the method (recomputes adapter_name locally). No renames, no signature changes to existing params, no new methods added. All logging.debug calls are guarded by . Ready for you to layer in idle_timeout_s/tick() - go ahead.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Debug logging done, purely additive, no control-flow changes. Summary: (1) added import logging + module-level _DEBUG_PREVIEW_MAX_LEN=80 + _truncate_preview() helper near top of file, after ADAPTER_SUPPORT_STREAMING. (2) __init__ signature: added debug: bool=False, debug_logger: logging.Logger or None=None params (appended after allow_reuse); added self.debug and self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger(dspy.streaming.listener) at end of __init__ body. (3) In receive(): guarded log inside the allow_reuse reset block (after the 5 state-clearing lines) logging State reset. (4) Guarded log right after self.stream_end = True in the cache_hit branch, logging Start detection. (5) Guarded log right after the JSONAdapter leading-quote-strip line inside the start_identifier-in-concat_message block, logging Start detection. (6) Guarded log right before self.stream_end = True in the rolling-end-check block, logging Rolling end check. (7) Guarded log right before the final return StreamResponse(...) in receive(), logging Emit chunk. (8) In flush(): changed the three adapter branches to assign to a local result variable instead of returning directly, then added a guarded log plus a single return result at the end of the method (recomputes adapter_name locally there). No renames, no changes to existing param signatures, no new methods added. All debug calls guarded by self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG). Ready for you to layer in idle_timeout_s and tick - go ahead.

note: Confirmed - looks like we are each working on separate copies of the repo (your idle_timeout_s/tick/import time changes are not present in my working tree even after your DONE). My debug-logging patch is finalized, tests pass (tests/streaming/ 15 passed 3 skipped), ruff clean, and patch.txt written. No further coordination needed on my end for this file.
